_comment: VA federal benefits by rating threshold. Benefits unlock at the listed threshold and remain available at all higher ratings. Cumulative display (0% through current rating) is handled in benefits_service.py.

healthcare.name: VA Healthcare Enrollment (Priority Group 8 / Cost-share)
healthcare.detail: Veterans with 0% rating may enroll in VA healthcare subject to income-based co-pays and Priority Group assignment.

insurance.name: VALife Insurance (up to $40,000)
insurance.detail: Open enrollment life insurance available to any veteran with a service-connected disability regardless of rating.

healthcare.name: VA Healthcare — Priority Group 3 (service-connected 10–20%)
healthcare.detail: Reduced or no copays for care related to your service-connected conditions.

healthcare.name: Prescription Medications — $5/30-day supply (SC conditions)
healthcare.detail: Co-pay of $5 per 30-day supply for medications related to service-connected conditions.

employment.name: Federal Hiring Preference — 10-Point Preference
employment.detail: 10-point veterans' preference on federal civil service exams and competitive job applications.

financial.name: Travel Pay (Beneficiary Travel)
financial.detail: Mileage reimbursement or special transportation to VA facilities for service-connected care.

housing.name: VA Home Loan Guarantee (reduced funding fee)
housing.detail: VA-backed home loan with competitive rates and no down payment required. Funding fee is reduced at 10%+.

financial.name: Clothing Allowance (if SC condition affects clothing)
financial.detail: Annual clothing allowance if your service-connected skin condition or prosthetic device damages or requires special clothing.

healthcare.name: VA Healthcare — Priority Group 2 (service-connected 30–49%)
healthcare.detail: Priority Group 2 with minimal copays. Dental care may be available.

healthcare.name: Comprehensive Dental Care (if rated 100% or hospitalized 90+ days)
healthcare.detail: Full VA dental eligibility begins at 100% or specific hospitalization criteria; 30% opens dental referral pathway.

employment.name: Vocational Rehabilitation & Employment (VR&E / Chapter 31)
employment.detail: Employment services, job training, education, and independent living services for veterans with at least 10% rating (most program depth at 20–30%+).

employment.name: Disabled Veteran Leave (federal employees — up to 104 hrs first year)
employment.detail: Federal employees who are veterans with a service-connected disability of 30%+ receive up to 104 hours of disabled veteran leave in their first year of employment.

financial.name: Concurrent Retirement and Disability Pay (CRDP)
financial.detail: Retired veterans with 50%+ rating receive CRDP. At 30–40%, CRDP phase-in applies for Chapter 61 retirees.

financial.name: Dependency and Indemnity Compensation (DIC) — survivor benefit
financial.detail: If veteran dies from a service-connected condition, surviving spouse/dependents may receive DIC payments. More likely established at 30%+.

financial.name: Dependents' add-on compensation (spouse, children, parents)
financial.detail: Monthly compensation increases when veteran has qualifying dependents. Available at 30%+. Rates vary by rating tier and number of dependents.

education.name: Dependents' Educational Assistance (DEA / Chapter 35)
education.detail: Education and training benefits for eligible dependents and survivors. Eligibility enhanced at 100% P&T but survivors of 30%+ SC veteran may qualify.

insurance.name: CHAMPVA (dependent healthcare) — at 100% P&T
insurance.detail: Civilian Health and Medical Program of the VA for dependents. Eligibility triggers at 100% P&T, but listed here as awareness item for progressing veterans.

healthcare.name: VA Healthcare — Priority Group 1 (service-connected 50–100%)
healthcare.detail: Highest priority group. Comprehensive care with no copays for all VA healthcare services.

healthcare.name: Medications — No copay (SC and non-SC conditions)
healthcare.detail: At 50%+, all VA prescriptions are provided at no cost.

healthcare.name: Community Care Network (CCN) — expanded access
healthcare.detail: Greater access to community (non-VA) providers when VA cannot provide timely or local care.

employment.name: Concurrent Retirement and Disability Pay (CRDP) — full eligibility
employment.detail: At 50%+, military retirees receive both retirement pay and VA disability compensation concurrently (no offset).

housing.name: VA Funding Fee Exemption (home loans)
housing.detail: Veterans receiving VA compensation at any rating are exempt from the VA home loan funding fee.

military.name: Commissary and Exchange (BX/PX) Privileges
military.detail: Service-connected veterans rated 0%+ may shop at military commissaries and exchanges on base.

military.name: MWR (Morale, Welfare & Recreation) Access
military.detail: Access to base recreational facilities, gyms, pools, and MWR programs.

financial.name: Total Disability / Individual Unemployability (TDIU)
financial.detail: If combined rating is 70%+ with one disability at 40%+, veteran may be eligible for TDIU — paid at 100% rate while rated less than 100%. File VA Form 21-8940.

healthcare.name: Full VA Dental Care
healthcare.detail: Comprehensive dental care at VA facilities at no cost for veterans rated 100% (schedular or TDIU).

healthcare.name: Caregiver Support Program
healthcare.detail: Enhanced eligibility for the Program of Comprehensive Assistance for Family Caregivers (PCAFC) at 100%.

employment.name: Non-Competitive Federal Employment (Schedule A hiring)
employment.detail: 100% disabled veterans may be appointed non-competitively to federal positions under Schedule A authority.

financial.name: Total and Permanent (P&T) Designation
financial.detail: 100% P&T veterans are protected from rating reduction and gain enhanced benefits eligibility including CHAMPVA and DEA.

financial.name: Property Tax Exemptions (state-specific)
financial.detail: Most states offer full or partial property tax exemptions for 100% disabled veterans. Check your state's veterans affairs office.

financial.name: Vehicle / License Plate Benefits (state-specific)
financial.detail: Many states offer free or discounted specialty disabled veteran license plates and vehicle registration at 100%.

financial.name: Total and Permanent Disability (TPD) Student Loan Discharge
financial.detail: 100% P&T veterans may qualify for discharge of federal student loans through the TPD discharge program.

education.name: Fry Scholarship (survivors of veterans who died in service/SC)
education.detail: Children and surviving spouses of veterans who died in the line of duty may receive up to 36 months of GI Bill-level education benefits.

education.name: Dependents' Educational Assistance (DEA / Chapter 35) — full eligibility
education.detail: Dependents of 100% P&T veterans receive full Chapter 35 education and training benefits.

education.name: Free Tuition — Many State Programs
education.detail: Most states offer free in-state tuition at public colleges/universities for 100% disabled veterans and/or their dependents.

housing.name: Specially Adapted Housing (SAH) Grant — up to $109,986
housing.detail: Grant to build, buy, or modify a home to meet the needs of certain veterans with service-connected disabilities.

housing.name: Special Home Adaptation (SHA) Grant — up to $22,036
housing.detail: Smaller adaptation grant for veterans with specific qualifying service-connected disabilities.

housing.name: Temporary Residence Adaptation (TRA) Grant
housing.detail: Allows SAH/SHA eligible veterans to apply grant funds to a temporary residence while their permanent home is being modified.

housing.name: Home Improvements and Structural Alterations (HISA) Grant
housing.detail: Up to $6,800 (SC) or $2,000 (non-SC) for medically necessary home improvements. Available below 100% but fully accessible at 100%.

insurance.name: CHAMPVA — Dependent Healthcare Coverage
insurance.detail: Comprehensive healthcare coverage for eligible dependents of 100% P&T veterans through the VA's CHAMPVA program.

insurance.name: Service-Disabled Veterans Life Insurance (S-DVI) waiver
insurance.detail: 100% totally disabled veterans may receive a waiver of premiums on S-DVI policies.

military.name: Space-A (Space-Available) Military Travel
military.detail: 100% disabled veterans and their dependents may fly on military aircraft on a space-available basis at no cost.

military.name: National Parks — America the Beautiful Access Pass (free)
military.detail: Free lifetime Access Pass for veterans with permanent disabilities providing free entry to all federal lands and recreation sites.
